concerns.text: Tasks T031-T034d implement a permutation baseline test and a halt-if-not-significant logic, but omit the explicit task to calculate and report the absolute cross-validated accuracy metric required by SC-003 (target >60%). The spec mandates measuring accuracy against a target, not just against a null distribution. Without a task to compute and log the absolute accuracy, SC-003 cannot be satisfied.

Task T021 (Shannon diversity) is listed as a separate step from T020 (CLR transformation) without explicit dependency ordering. The spec (US-2) and FR-002 require CLR transformation to be applied *before* calculating diversity metrics to address compositional constraints. The current task list risks implementing Shannon on raw relative abundances, violating the scientific constraint.

Task T014 ('Add logic to handle subjects with titers below limit of detection') is semantically incomplete. The spec (Edge Cases) requires a specific choice: impute to half detection limit OR exclude, with the choice documented. The task does not mandate which method to implement, leaving the acceptance criterion (documented choice) unverified and the implementation ambiguous.

Tasks T034a-T034d focus entirely on comparing the model against a permutation baseline and halting if p < 0.05. They do not include a step to verify the model's accuracy against the SC-003 target of >60%. The spec requires measuring accuracy against a target; the tasks only measure against a null hypothesis. This is a silent relaxation of the success criterion.

Tasks T011b-T011d implement a full raw FASTQ processing pipeline (Strategy B) as a mandatory fallback. While the plan mentions this as a fallback, the spec (FR-001) and plan (Risk Assessment) assume pre-processed data is available. Implementing a full QIIME2/DADA2 pipeline in the tasks without a conditional 'if Strategy A fails' guard in the task description itself risks scope creep and violates the 'pre-processed' assumption if not strictly gated.
